title: Workday: Buy While It's Quietly Hitting Targets
text: Shares of Workday have declined 40% this year, despite consistent fundamental performance. Despite its ~$6 billion annual revenue run rate, the company continues to grow at a ~20% y/y pace, which is unusual for a company of its size.

title: Sequoia's Carl Eschenbach, who led deals for Zoom and Snowflake, to run Workday as co-CEO
text: Carl Eschenbach, a longtime enterprise software executive who joined Sequoia Capital in 2016 and went on to lead a number of lucrative deals for the venture firm, is going back to an operating role.

title: Workday names Sequoia's Carl Eschenbach as co-CEO, alongside Aneel Bhusri
text: Workday named Carl Eschenbach, who spent almost seven years at Sequoia Capital, as its new co-CEO, replacing Chano Fernandez.

title: Workday appoints Carl Eschenbach as co-CEO
text: Workday Inc. WDAY, +1.21% said late Tuesday it appointed Carl Eschenbach to serve as co-chief executive along with co-founder and chairman Aneel Bhusri effective immediately. Eschenbach is a partner at private equity firm Sequoia Capital, and will stay with the firm in a “venture partner capacity.

title: Workday: I Called This Wrong, Why I'm Bullish
text: Workday was a stock that I was bearish on, as I believed that its operations would struggle over the next twelve months. But its guidance for fiscal 2024 points to yet another year of strong revenue growth.
